reasoning: Query asks for Tohoku sakura spots (Spanish). Tool fetched only Akita generic spots — no sakura_meisho_100 filter applied, returning castles, lakes, volcanoes and fireworks. Yoshino is Nara not Tohoku. Data is authoritative but misses the sakura filter across all 6 Tohoku prefectures. Rule C: single-prefecture-only = scope gap.
missing_data: Sakura-specific spots (sakura_meisho_100 kind) across all Tohoku prefectures: Hirosaki Castle, Kakunodate, Kitakami Tenshochi, Funaoka Castle, etc. Response has zero sakura_meisho_100 entries in the visible portion.
improvement_hint: Filter by kinds=sakura_meisho_100 and fan out to all 6 Tohoku prefectures (Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Akita, Yamagata, Fukushima). A region_fanout for 'Tohoku' would solve this.

reasoning: Tool returns 24 items of Niigata sake brands and GI records including the major three (Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota). Notable caveat: response mixes GI food items (edamame, etc.) not directly sake-brewery related. English lang respected. Authoritative MAFF + Wikidata sources.
missing_data: Physical brewery addresses and visiting info (tours/tastings). The query asked for 'major sake breweries' suggesting visitable venues; only brand/product records returned.
improvement_hint: Add a see_also_wikidata_venues section for brewery (sake_brewery kind) spots in Niigata with visiting metadata.

reasoning: Query is in French; response is in English/Japanese. Rule D: constraint_handling -1. Also the query asks for ski resorts WITH onsen in Hakuba — only Happo-One (ski) returned, no onsen records. Data is authoritative but partial: ski resort present, onsen absent. Rule B: primary failure is constraint (language + content filter).
missing_data: Onsen spots/ryokan in Hakuba area (e.g. Hakuba Happo Onsen, Kamishiro Onsen). No hot spring data returned despite explicit dual constraint.
improvement_hint: Co-query get_hotels with onsen_ryokan filter for 白馬 municipality alongside get_spots. French language should propagate to description fields.

reasoning: Query asks for Kansai autumn foliage spots. Tool returned Kyoto generic spots with no koyo/autumn filter — top results are Nijo Castle, Toji Temple, Nagaoka-kyo (archaeological site). None specifically flagged for autumn foliage. Kansai is multi-prefecture (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka, Hyogo, etc.) but only Kyoto fetched. Rule C: authoritative data but wrong-scope filter and missing foliage-specific filtering.
missing_data: Koyo-specific spots (e.g. Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji, Arashiyama in Kyoto; Yoshino/Nara; Minoo Park/Osaka). No autumn-foliage kind filter applied, and Kansai fan-out to Nara/Osaka/Hyogo missing.
improvement_hint: Filter by kinds containing koyo or momiji-themed tags; apply region fan-out for Kansai (prefectures 26,27,28,29,30). Seasonal relevance ranking would help.

reasoning: Query is in Arabic; response is in English/Japanese — Rule D: constraint_handling -1 from otherwise-deserved 3. The top result is Yoshino (sakura_meisho_100) which is excellent. However scope is Nara only, not all Kansai (query says 'Kansai'). Authoritative data present. Double failure: language mismatch + geographic scope. Rule B: language is the single worst dim.
missing_data: Kansai-wide sakura spots beyond Nara: Maruyama Park (Kyoto), Himeji Castle (Hyogo), Osaka Castle park. Arabic description/name fields absent.
improvement_hint: Apply Kansai region fan-out (26-30). Support Arabic in description fields or add ar to lang parameter handling.

reasoning: Query asks for 'remote Okinawan islands with beautiful starry skies'. Response returns generic Okinawa spots — castles (Nakagusuku, Zakimi), Tamaudun mausoleum, Kumejima town entry. No dark_sky kind or island-specific filter applied. Kumejima appears but only as a municipality entry, not as a dark-sky observation spot. Critical retrieval miss on the dark_sky / stargazing intent.
missing_data: Dark sky spots on Okinawan remote islands: Iriomote-jima, Taketomi-jima, Miyako-jima. dark_sky kind entries, or at minimum islands tagged for low light pollution.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky kind tag to appropriate Okinawan island spots. Filter by kinds=dark_sky or island when stargazing intent detected. Municipality fan-out to離島 municipalities.

reasoning: Query is in Thai. Response is in Japanese/English — Rule D constraint_handling hit. Content is good: hito_yu_kai members in rural Hokkaido (知内温泉, 銀婚湯, 十勝岳温泉, 旭岳温泉) genuinely match 'rural onsen'. Hotel data is authoritative. But ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo (urban) appears diluting the rural focus. Language mismatch is the primary failure.
missing_data: Thai-language descriptions or names. Coordinates missing for hito_yu_kai entries, limiting map-based usability.
improvement_hint: Add Thai (th) to supported lang codes for hotel description translation. Add rural/remote filter based on municipality population or hito_yu_kai designation as proxy for rural character.

reasoning: Query asks for islands in the Seto Inland Sea (Vietnamese). Tool returned Kagawa general spots — Ritsurin Garden, Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle — all mainland Takamatsu/Marugame. The Seto Inland Sea islands (Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, etc.) are absent. Scope is mainland Kagawa, not the islands. Rule C does not rescue: the relevant entity class (islands) is entirely absent.
missing_data: Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Ogijima — the core Setouchi island destinations. Island kind filter and fan-out to Hiroshima/Okayama Setouchi islands also missing.
improvement_hint: Add island kind tag to Seto Inland Sea islands and enable cross-prefecture region fan-out for 'Setouchi'. Filter by kinds=island when island-visit intent detected.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops. Tool returned Tokushima craft METI records. 阿波正藍しじら織 (Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave) is an indigo textile craft — relevant. But Awa Washi (washi paper) and Otani-yaki (ceramics) are not indigo-related. The response returns 3 crafts of which only 1 is truly indigo. No workshop/venue data for hands-on experience. Reasoning failure: data present but only partially aligned.
missing_data: Indigo dyeing (藍染) hands-on workshop venues in Tokushima. The craft record for 阿波藍 as a dyeing tradition specifically (separate from shijira weave) is not present.
improvement_hint: Add keyword 'ai' or '藍' filter in addition to category filter. Add workshop-type venue records from municipal scrape for Tokushima indigo experience facilities.

reasoning: Query (German) asks for shukubo on Mt. Koya. Response returns multiple shukubo from Koyasan Shukubo Association, all in 高野町 — exact match. German language not served in description but content is on-target. Rule D: language mismatch would drop constraint_handling but the query's primary constraint (Koya shukubo) is perfectly met; lang is a secondary constraint. Notable caveat: no coordinates or website for most entries.
missing_data: Coordinates and website URLs for most shukubo entries. German-language descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Enrich shukubo entries with coordinates and websites from Koyasan Shukubo Association site. Add German (de) to supported translation langs.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returns 153 matches including Kakunodate (Akita), Fukui samurai residence, Matsue (Shimane). Notable caveat: first results include a Tokyo buke_yashiki gate which is inside Tokyo (not 'off the beaten path') and one result labeled Kyoto would be noise. Mostly useful multi-prefecture coverage.
missing_data: Chiran (Kagoshima), Kitsuki (Oita), Hagi (Yamaguchi) samurai districts. Score could improve with 'exclude Kyoto/Tokyo' negative filter support.
improvement_hint: Support negative prefecture filter in search_area to exclude Tokyo/Kyoto when query contains 'outside'. Boost preservation_district + buke_yashiki combo items.

reasoning: Query asks for summer festivals in Akita (Indonesian). Response returns only 3 festivals (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shinmei) from bunka_intangible — these are important intangible folk arts, not summer matsuri specifically. The most famous Akita summer festival, Kanto Matsuri (竿燈まつり), is absent. Indonesian lang served at low confidence for one entry. Coverage too sparse for 'festivals'.
missing_data: 竿燈まつり (Akita Kanto Festival, one of Japan's top 3 summer festivals), Omagari Fireworks. These are the canonical Akita summer events and should appear.
improvement_hint: Scrape Schema.org events from Akita prefectural tourism site to capture summer festival data. Add scraped_schema_event source to get_festivals alongside bunka_intangible.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns in Sanin region (Korean). Tool returned Shimane general spots — Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine, Mt. Sanbe — no fishing village or port town. Korean lang not in descriptions. The specific entity class (漁港・漁村) is entirely absent from visible results. Iwami Ginzan is inland, not a fishing port.
missing_data: Fishing port towns: Onomichi, Yurihama, Tottori coast towns, Izumo coast. Spots with kinds=fishing_village or coast/harbor types in Tottori and Shimane.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_village / fishing_port kind tags to relevant coastal municipalities. Fan-out to Tottori (the other Sanin prefecture). Korean descriptions need enrichment.

reasoning: Query asks for washi paper-making experience in Gifu. Tool returned 8 Gifu crafts: Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquer), wood crafts — and only implicitly contains Mino Washi. No washi-specific item visible in the 4000-char truncated response. The query intent (washi experience) is partially addressed via crafts tool but without keyword filtering. Rule C: authoritative data present but wrong-filter — washi not surfaced prominently.
missing_data: Mino Washi (美濃和紙) METI craft record and workshop venues. The craft record exists but was not surfaced at top due to lack of keyword filter.
improvement_hint: Apply q='washi' keyword filter in get_local_specialty alongside category filter. Alternatively add see_also_wikidata_venues for washi workshop spots in Gifu (Mino City).

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula (Spanish). Tool returned Ishikawa general spots — Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Oyama Shrine — all in Kanazawa city, not Noto. The Noto Peninsula fishing villages (Wajima, Suzu, Monzen) are absent. municipality='能登' or Noto-specific filtering was not applied. Relevant entity class entirely absent from results.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula fishing villages: Wajima (輪島), Suzu (珠洲), Monzen, Noto-cho coast. Should filter by municipality targeting Noto region, not Kanazawa.
improvement_hint: Add Noto Peninsula sub-region filter (municipalities: Wajima, Suzu, Noto-machi, Anamizu). Fishing village kind tags needed for coastal Ishikawa communities.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes in Japan beyond Tottori. search_area returns 91 matches including Fukiagehama (Kagoshima), Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari Local Museum (Hokkaido sand dune museum). Notable caveat: Tottori dunes appear twice at top and the query explicitly asked beyond Tottori — no negative filter support. But other dunes are present. Good multi-prefecture coverage.
missing_data: Sakata sand dunes (Shonai Coast, Yamagata), Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Hamamatsu). Some entries lack coordinates.
improvement_hint: Implement negative toponym filter: when query says 'not X' or 'beyond X', deprioritize/exclude matching results. Boost non-Tottori sand_dune results.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for Sanin region traditional crafts. Tool returns Shimane crafts (4 items) in Chinese: Iwami Yaki, Sekishu Washi, Unshu Soroban, Izumo Stone Lantern. Chinese descriptions present and accurate. Notable caveat: Sanin is two prefectures (Shimane + Tottori) but only Shimane fetched. Rule C: one-prefecture coverage for two-prefecture region.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts (Inshū Washi, Tottori mingei). Sanin region fan-out to Tottori not applied.
improvement_hint: Support Sanin region code to fan-out to both Shimane and Tottori when region_fanout is triggered. Add region='sanin' as named region in the region taxonomy.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool returned generic Hokkaido spots — Showa-Shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle — no lavender_field kind present. The lavender_route or flower_garden kind was not filtered. missing_data: Lavender fields: Tomita Farm (Furano, as reference), Higashimokoto Shibazakura Park, Biei Shikisai-no-Oka, Kamifurano lavender spots. kinds=lavender_field or flower_garden filter needed.

reasoning: Query in Filipino asks for traditional old-town townscapes ('bayan ng kape') in Kyushu — likely 'kampe' meaning old-town or historical streets. Tool returned Oita generic spots: Usuki Castle, Usa Jingū, Oka Castle. These are individual attractions, not preserved old-town streetscapes. Only Oita fetched vs. all Kyushu. The preserved townscape entity class (machinami, preservation_district) is absent.
missing_data: Kyushu preserved old-town districts: Yanagawa (Fukuoka), Hita Mameda-machi (Oita), Usuki machinami, Nagasaki Dejima. Cross-prefecture Kyushu fan-out needed.

reasoning: Query asks for stayable kominka (old farmhouses) in the Hokuriku region. Tool returned Ishikawa hotels — onsen ryokan (hito_yu_kai) and standard hotels including a 'Student housing 1' OSM entry with no name. No kominka/machiya lodging type returned. The relevant lodging category (kominka) is entirely absent. Also Hokuriku = Ishikawa+Toyama+Fukui but only Ishikawa fetched.
missing_data: Kominka guesthouses in Hokuriku region: Shirakawa-go/Gokayama (Toyama/Gifu border) gasshō-zukuri, Noto kominka stays, Fukui machiya. No kominka lodging_type in response.
wrong_data: Student housing 1 (OSM node 5439110525) is noise — not a travel accommodation.
improvement_hint: Add kominka / machiya as lodging_type filters. Fan-out to Toyama and Fukui for Hokuriku region. Filter out student_housing OSM nodes from hotel results.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. Response from get_japan_heritage returns Kongōbu-ji, Kumano Kodō, Kumano Sanzan — all highly relevant to Kii mountain sacred site network. Good. Notable caveat: the tool_scope_note correctly flags UNESCO distinction. Query intent well addressed. Minor: Tottori is included in prefecture_codes (prefecture 30=Wakayama, correct).
missing_data: Nachi Taisha and Hayatama Taisha individually named. Yoshino sacred peaks (Omine). Mie prefecture Kumano sites (cross-prefecture).
improvement_hint: Fan-out Kii Peninsula heritage to include Mie (24) and Nara (29) prefectures for full Kumano coverage. Surface individual shrine entities alongside grouped heritage narratives.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns 129 Niigata festivals — includes 長岡まつり大花火大会 (one of Japan's top fireworks), which is excellent. However coverage is only Niigata; Sea of Japan coast spans Akita, Yamagata, Ishikawa, Fukui too. First two results are spring flower events (芝桜/ヒメサユリ) not fireworks. Good but single-prefecture and no fireworks filter applied.
missing_data: Sea of Japan coast fireworks beyond Niigata: Akita Ōmagari fireworks, Tsuruga fireworks (Fukui), Yoshida fireworks (Shizuoka coast). Cross-prefecture Sea of Japan fan-out needed.
improvement_hint: Add fireworks kind filter (hanabi) to get_festivals. Support Sea of Japan coast region fan-out (Niigata+Akita+Yamagata+Ishikawa+Fukui+Toyama).

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Response returns 2 items: Beppu Bamboo Crafts (perfect match, Oita-only production area) and Hakata Ori (weaving, multi-prefecture including Oita). Beppu Bamboo Crafts entry has excellent description with technique details. Notable caveat: Hakata Ori is not bamboo — it's silk weaving — though its prefecture_codes includes 44 (Oita). Minor relevance dilution but bamboo craft is the #1 result.
missing_data: Workshop/museum venues for Beppu bamboo crafts experience (hands-on). Oita Prefectural Museum of History appears in see_also but bamboo-specific venues not surfaced.
improvement_hint: Add see_also_wikidata_venues entries specifically for bamboo craft workshops/studios in Beppu. Keyword filter 'bamboo' or '竹' would help exclude non-bamboo crafts.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes in rural Shikoku. Ōmishima island (cycling_route + walking_road_500 kinds) is present — relevant. But top results are castles (Ōzu, Yuzuki, Uwajima) with no cycling relevance. Only Ehime fetched; Shikoku = 4 prefectures (Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa too). Shimanami Kaidō (the world-famous Shikoku cycling route) should be the top result. Coverage too sparse for the full Shikoku cycling query.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaidō (cycling_route kind), Shimanto River cycling route (Kochi), Yoshino River cycling (Tokushima). Cross-prefecture Shikoku fan-out needed.
improvement_hint: Filter by kinds=cycling_route when cycling intent detected. Apply Shikoku region fan-out (Ehime+Kochi+Tokushima+Kagawa). Shimanami Kaidō should have high prominence_score.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo/Kyoto. get_traditional_arts returns 183 items — kabuki (Q199701) is present as item 2 but it is a national-level art form entry with no venue/location data. Bunraku, Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku all appear before regional kabuki entries. No venue-level data (specific theaters outside Tokyo/Kyoto) surfaced. Data is authoritative but buried — ranking failure.
missing_data: Regional kabuki venues: Kanamaru-za (Kotohira, Kagawa), Uchiko-za (Ehime), Chichibu Yomatsuri. Venue-level kabuki records with addresses and performance schedules.
improvement_hint: Add venue-level kabuki theater records to get_traditional_arts see_also. Keyword filter on 'kabuki' should surface kabuki_theater kind spots with geographic data prioritized.
